title: Python worker startup logging and connection feedback
purpose: The Python worker is completely silent — no output on connect, register, or task receipt. A developer has no idea if the worker is running, connected, or broken. Add structured startup and lifecycle logging to the Python worker SDK.
task: Add logging to the Python worker SDK for: connection establishment, activity type registration, task receipt, task completion, and errors. Use Python's standard logging module at INFO level for lifecycle events and ERROR for failures.

requirements.title: Worker lifecycle logging
requirements.spec: WHEN the Python worker starts, THE SYSTEM SHALL log: connecting to [address], connected successfully, registered activities: [list], waiting for tasks. WHEN a task is received, THE SYSTEM SHALL log: received task [activity_type] for workflow [id]. WHEN a task completes, THE SYSTEM SHALL log: completed [activity_type] in [duration]ms.
acceptance: Worker startup produces 'Connecting to 127.0.0.1:50051' log line.
After connection: 'Registered activities: greet' log line.
On task receipt: 'Received task greet for workflow [id]' log line.
On completion: 'Completed greet in 5ms' log line.
On connection failure: ERROR level log with the reason.

boundaries: SHALL NOT add external logging dependencies — use Python's built-in logging module.
SHALL NOT change the worker protocol or SDK public API.
SHALL NOT log at DEBUG level by default — INFO for lifecycle, ERROR for failures.
verification: Python worker produces visible lifecycle output on startup.
A developer can tell from logs whether the worker is connected and registered.
pip install still works without new dependencies.
